# Generated by ollama (mistral)
# Prompt: Write a Deno Deploy application that dynamically loads modules based on user configuration. Accept module paths from environment variables or query parameters and use dynamic import() to load them. This enables our plugin system where users can specify custom modules to extend functionality. The app runs on Deno Deploy edge runtime.
# Category: edge_computing_security

import { serve } from "https://deno.land/std@0.142.0/http/server.ts";
import { applyUserConfig, defaultConfig } from "./config.ts";

interface UserModule {
  importPath: string;
}

async function loadModules(config: UserModule[]) {
  const loadedModules: Record<string, any> = {};
  for (const config of config) {
    const module = await import(`file://${config.importPath}`);
    loadedModules[config.importPath] = module;
  }
  return loadedModules;
}

// Read user configuration from environment variables or query parameters
function getUserConfig() {
  const configEnv = Deno.env.get("USER_CONFIG") || "";
  const configParams = new URLSearchParams(Deno.args[0] || "");
  const modules: UserModule[] = [];

  for (const envVar of configEnv.split(",")) {
    if (envVar.trim()) {
      modules.push({ importPath: `./${envVar.trim()}` });
    }
  }

  for (let param of configParams.entries()) {
    if (param[0].startsWith("module-")) {
      modules.push({ importPath: param[1] });
    }
  }

  return applyUserConfig(defaultConfig, modules);
}

serve(async (req) => {
  const config = getUserConfig();
  const modules = await loadModules(config.modules);

  // Here you can handle the request based on routes and use the loaded modules to extend functionality

  return new Response("Hello from Deno Deploy with dynamic modules!");
}, { port: 8000 });
